mnemonics: Make Kool Directories In Recursive fashion
keyPoints: Used to create a new directory.
Requires appropriate permissions in the parent directory.
The -p flag creates parent directories recursively if they don't exist.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to include necessary permissions (read/write/execute) in the parent directory.
Assuming the command will create intermediate directories without using the -p flag.
Using invalid characters in the directory name (e.g., spaces without proper quoting).
